domains.name: Business
domains.description: The fundamentals of running and understanding a company.

topics.title: Finance & Accounting Fundamentals
topics.notes: Learn to read a balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement before anything else.

resources.title: Visible.vc: How to Calculate Runway & Burn Rate
April Dunford: An Introduction to Positioning
How to Nail Your Product Positioning | April Dunford (Lenny's Podcast)

topics.title: Sales & Negotiation
topics.notes: As a technical founder you'll be doing sales yourself before you can hire for it. Start with founder-led sales.

resources.title: Never Split the Difference - Chris Voss | Talks at Google
Josh Braun on Cold Emails That Get Responses
First Round Review: Founder-Led Sales
SaaStr: 7 Things All Founders Should Know About Sales (w/ Dave Kellogg)
Lenny's Newsletter: Good Strategy, Bad Strategy (Richard Rumelt)
Manager Tools: The Kernel of Strategy

domains.name: Computer Science
domains.description: The theory behind how software and computers actually work.

resources.title: Big-O Cheat Sheet

topics.title: Computer Systems & Databases
topics.notes: CMU's 15-445/645, one specific lecture per unit rather than the whole playlist.

resources.title: CMU 15-445: Storage Models & Compression
CMU 15-445: Vector Indexes, Inverted Indexes & Filters
CMU 15-445: Query Planning & Optimization
CMU 15-445: Concurrency Control Theory
CMU 15-445: Database Recovery with ARIES

topics.title: Machine Learning Fundamentals
topics.notes: Google's Crash Course, split into its real units — go through them in order.

resources.title: ML Crash Course: Linear Regression
ML Crash Course: Logistic Regression
ML Crash Course: Classification
ML Crash Course: Working with Numerical Data
ML Crash Course: Working with Categorical Data
ML Crash Course: Datasets, Generalization & Overfitting
ML Crash Course: Neural Networks
ML Crash Course: Embeddings
ML Crash Course: Intro to Large Language Models
ML Crash Course: Production ML Systems
ML Crash Course: AutoML
ML Crash Course: ML Fairness
StatQuest: Linear Regression, Clearly Explained!!!
Jay Alammar: The Illustrated Transformer

topics.title: Neural Network Intuition
topics.notes: 3Blue1Brown's series — the visual/intuitive layer on top of the Crash Course above.

resources.title: But What Is a Neural Network?
Gradient Descent, How Neural Networks Learn
What Is Backpropagation Really Doing?
Backpropagation Calculus
But What Is a GPT? Visual Intro to Transformers
Attention in Transformers, Visually Explained
How Might LLMs Store Facts?

topics.title: Practical Deep Learning (fast.ai)
topics.notes: Part 1 of fast.ai's course — hands-on and code-first, the complement to the more theoretical resources above.

resources.title: fast.ai Lesson 1: Getting Started
fast.ai Lesson 2: Deployment
fast.ai Lesson 3: Neural Net Foundations
fast.ai Lesson 4: Natural Language (NLP)
fast.ai Lesson 5: From-Scratch Model
fast.ai Lesson 6: Random Forests
fast.ai Lesson 7: Collaborative Filtering
fast.ai Lesson 8: Convolutions (CNNs)

topics.title: Building Neural Nets From Scratch (Karpathy)
topics.notes: The most hands-on of the four — you write the code yourself, line by line.

resources.title: The Spelled-Out Intro to Neural Networks: Building micrograd
The Spelled-Out Intro to Language Modeling: Building makemore
Building makemore Part 2: MLP
Building makemore Part 3: Activations & Gradients, BatchNorm
Building makemore Part 4: Becoming a Backprop Ninja
Building makemore Part 5: Building a WaveNet
Let's Build GPT: From Scratch, in Code, Spelled Out
Let's Build the GPT Tokenizer

domains.name: Technology
domains.description: Practical computer science for building and running real systems: code, cloud, networking, and security.

topics.title: Software Engineering Practices
topics.notes: freeCodeCamp's certification pages aren't individually linkable, so these are single articles covering the same JS fundamentals, one topic each.

resources.title: JavaScript Basics
ES6 In Depth: An Introduction
Regular Expressions
What Went Wrong? Troubleshooting JavaScript
Indexed Collections
Introduction to Algorithms Handbook — with JavaScript Examples
Object-Oriented Programming
What Is Functional Programming? A Beginner's JavaScript Guide
Top 10 JavaScript Algorithms for Coding Challenges
The Standard of Code Review

topics.title: Cloud & Infrastructure
topics.notes: AWS Skill Builder's course modules aren't individually linkable, so these are single AWS docs/explainer pages, one topic each.

resources.title: What Is Cloud Computing?
What Is Amazon EC2?
AWS Global Infrastructure
What Is Amazon VPC?
What Is Cloud Storage?
AWS Shared Responsibility Model
What Is Amazon CloudWatch?
The Twelve-Factor App

topics.title: Cybersecurity Fundamentals
topics.notes: The practical basics: the standard list of what actually goes wrong, plus the mechanisms (encryption, hashing, phishing) behind it.

resources.title: OWASP Top 10:2021
Cloudflare: What Is a Firewall?
Cloudflare: What Is Encryption?
Cloudflare: What Is Phishing?
Public Key Cryptography - Computerphile
How NOT to Store Passwords! - Computerphile

topics.title: Networking Fundamentals
topics.notes: How data actually gets from one machine to another — the layer underneath every cloud and security topic above.

resources.title: Cloudflare: What Is DNS?
Cloudflare: What Is TCP/IP?
ByteByteGo: What Is the OSI Model?
What Happens When You Type google.com and Press Enter

domains.name: Biotech
domains.description: Enough biology, drug development, and biotech business literacy to walk into a biotech startup and be useful fast.

topics.title: Biology & Life Sciences Fundamentals
topics.notes: Start here if it's been a while since biology class — everything else in this domain assumes this vocabulary.

resources.title: MIT 7.01SC: Biochemical Reactions, Enzymes, and ATP
MIT 7.01SC: DNA Replication
MIT 7.01SC: Mendel's Laws
MIT 7.01SC: Development of Recombinant DNA

topics.title: Drug Discovery & Development Pipeline
topics.notes: Know the shape of the pipeline (discovery → preclinical → Phase I/II/III → FDA review) before your first week at a biotech startup.

resources.title: FDA: The Drug Development Process

topics.title: Computational Biology & Bioinformatics
topics.notes: Directly useful given the protein-function-classifier work — this is where ML and biotech actually meet.

resources.title: DeepMind: AlphaFold Reveals the Structure of the Protein Universe
Fundamentals of Bioinformatics: Biological Information (Part 1)
Fundamentals of Bioinformatics: Pairwise Sequence Alignment (Part 1)
Fundamentals of Bioinformatics: Sequence Homology Searching (Part 1)
Fundamentals of Bioinformatics: Microbiome Analysis Methods (Part 1)
Fundamentals of Bioinformatics: Machine Learning in Bioinformatics (Part 1)

topics.title: Biotech Business, VC & Deals
topics.notes: Biotech financing, licensing, and M&A run on different logic than software startups — don't assume SaaS playbooks transfer.

resources.title: LifeSciVC: Process-Driven Cadence: Biotech IPOs Demystified
a16z: The Biggest Company in the World

topics.title: Idea Validation & Customer Discovery
topics.notes: Talk to users before writing code — the single highest-leverage habit for a first-time founder.

resources.title: Y Combinator: How to Get Your First Customers | Startup School
How to Start a Startup, Lecture 1: How to Start a Startup (Sam Altman)
How to Start a Startup, Lecture 2: Ideas, Products, Teams and Execution (Sam Altman)
How to Start a Startup, Lecture 4: Building Product, Talking to Users, and Growing (Adora Cheung)
Y Combinator: A Guide to Seed Fundraising
Sequoia Capital: Writing a Business Plan
Guy Kawasaki: The Only 10 Slides You Need in Your Pitch
Kevin Hale (YC): How to Pitch Your Startup
First Round Review: The Fundraising Wisdom That Helped Our Founders Raise $18B in Follow-On Capital
Lenny's Newsletter: Scaling Your B2B Growth Engine
Lenny's Podcast: 10 Growth Tactics That Never Work | Elena Verna
First Round Review: You're Not Ready for a Head of Growth — Run This Founder-Led Growth Playbook Instead
Paul Graham: Startups in 13 Sentences
First Round Review: My Lessons from Interviewing 400+ Engineers Over Three Startups
First Round Review: Read This Before Joining as Employee 1 to 20

topics.title: Networking & the Bay Area Ecosystem
topics.notes: Added this one deliberately: as a technical founder here, who you know shapes your fundraising, hiring, and early customers as much as what you build.

resources.title: First Round Review: Don't Just Network — Build Your 'Meaningful Network' to Maximize Your Impact
Forbes: 4 Tactics for Improving Your Networking Skills as a Startup Founder
Fortune: Startup Founders Turn to These Networking Groups to Rub Shoulders with Their Peers
